name: Stuck in the Mud
setup: Mark out 20x20m square. Pick 2 catchers.
instructions: Catchers try to tag other players
Tagged players freeze with legs apart (stuck in mud)
Free players can crawl through frozen players' legs to free them
If all players frozen, catchers win — pick new catchers and go again
Play 2-3 minute rounds
coachingPoints: Keep head up while running
Use quick direction changes to avoid catchers
Encourage teamwork (freeing teammates)

benefits.immediate: Raises heart rate, develops agility and spatial awareness
benefits.longTerm: Builds teamwork, decision-making under pressure, and movement off the ball

name: Traffic Lights
setup: Each player with ball in 20x20m grid.
instructions: Players dribble around grid
Coach calls colors: Green = dribble, Amber = slow down (walk pace), Red = stop (foot on ball)
Add variations: Blue = change direction, Purple = perform a skill move
coachingPoints: Keep ball close when dribbling
Use different parts of foot
Head up to avoid collisions

benefits.immediate: Ball control practice, listening skills, reaction speed
benefits.longTerm: Close control in tight spaces, awareness of surroundings

name: Dribbling Relay
setup: Split into teams of 3-4. Set up cone courses (zig-zag or straight with turn point).
instructions: First player dribbles through cones to end point and back
Tag next teammate who goes
First team with all players finished wins
Repeat with variations (left foot only, specific skill moves)
coachingPoints: Keep ball under control (speed vs. control balance)
Use outside of foot around cones
Accelerate in open space

benefits.immediate: Dribbling technique, competitive fun, speed with ball
benefits.longTerm: Confidence on ball, ability to beat defenders with pace and control

name: Keep-Ball Circle
setup: Circle of players (8-10m diameter), 2-3 defenders in middle.
instructions: Outside players pass ball across circle
Defenders try to intercept
If defender wins ball or it goes outside circle, player who made mistake swaps in
Progress: limit touches (2-touch, then 1-touch)
coachingPoints: Look before receiving
Pass to feet with right weight
Defenders press quickly after pass

benefits.longTerm: Composure in possession, quick decision-making

name: 1v1 Channel Defending
setup: 5m wide x 15m long channels. Attacker starts at one end with ball, defender at other.
instructions: Attacker tries to dribble through channel past defender
Defender aims to win ball or force attacker out of bounds
Rotate roles after each attempt
Progression: widen channel or add small goals to attack/defend
coachingPoints: Defender: get side-on (not square), force attacker one way
Stay on feet (don't dive in)
Jockey backwards at speed of attacker

benefits.longTerm: Defensive confidence, ability to delay attackers and win ball cleanly

name: Pressing in Pairs
setup: 20x15m grid. Two defenders vs. 4-5 attackers who keep possession.
instructions: Attackers try to keep ball with passes
Defenders work as pair to win ball (one pressures ball, one covers)
If defenders win ball, they pass to coach and attackers restart
Rotate defenders every 90 seconds
coachingPoints: Press together (don't leave partner isolated)
Closest defender pressures ball, partner cuts passing lane
Communicate (call 'press' or 'cover')

benefits.longTerm: Understanding defensive partnerships and compactness

name: Protect the Castle
setup: 4 small grids (10x10m). Each grid has defender protecting 'castle' (cone or bottle in center) vs. 2-3 attackers.
instructions: Attackers try to knock over castle by passing ball into it
Defender blocks shots and clears ball
Rotate roles every 2 minutes
Progression: add second defender
coachingPoints: Defender: stay between ball and castle
Close down shooter quickly
Block with body or feet

benefits.immediate: Blocking shots, quick reactions, protective positioning
benefits.longTerm: Defensive positioning, bravery in blocks, goalkeeping basics

name: Transition: Win Ball → Attack
setup: 30x20m pitch with goal at each end. 5v5 or 6v6.
instructions: Defending team tries to win ball
Once won, immediately counter-attack opposite goal
Losing team must recover and defend
Goal = restart with other team in possession
coachingPoints: Win ball and look forward immediately
Support ball-winner quickly (get ahead of ball)
Transition from defending to attacking mindset instantly

benefits.longTerm: Game intelligence, recognizing moments to attack after winning ball

name: Defensive Shape (Small-Sided Game)
setup: 40x30m pitch. 6v6 or 7v7. Mark defensive line with cones (halfway line).
instructions: Normal game but defending team must stay compact (within 10m of each other)
If defenders too spread out, coach stops play
Attackers try to stretch defenders and exploit gaps
coachingPoints: Defenders: squeeze together when ball is far side
Shift as unit left/right with ball movement
Don't chase ball individually — move as team

benefits.immediate: Team defending shape, compactness, collective movement
benefits.longTerm: Tactical understanding of defensive structure in real games

name: Tackling Technique Stations
setup: 3 stations (5 mins each, rotate): (1) Block tackle vs. static partner, (2) Poke tackle in channel 1v1, (3) Jockeying practice.
instructions: Station 1: Players face each other with ball between, both try to win cleanly
Station 2: Defender uses poke tackle to knock ball away in narrow channel
Station 3: Defender jockeys attacker backwards without diving in
coachingPoints: Get low, strong body position
Time tackle when ball is away from attacker's foot
Stay balanced (don't lunge and lose shape)

benefits.immediate: Technical tackling skills, timing, body strength
benefits.longTerm: Confidence to tackle fairly and effectively without fouling

name: 2v1 Overload Practice
setup: 15x10m grids. 2 attackers vs. 1 defender. Goal at one end.
instructions: Attackers try to score past defender
Use width and quick passing to create space
Rotate: scorer becomes defender, defender joins queue
coachingPoints: Spread wide (make defender choose)
Pass and move into space
Shoot quickly when chance appears

benefits.immediate: Creating and exploiting overload situations, support play
benefits.longTerm: Recognizing numerical advantage in games and using it effectively

name: Finishing Circuit
setup: 3-4 stations approaching goal from different angles.
instructions: Station 1: Dribble around cone, shoot near post
Station 2: Receive pass, one-touch finish
Station 3: Dribble and shoot far post
Station 4: Receive and turn, then shoot
Rotate stations every 3 minutes
coachingPoints: Keep head steady when shooting
Strike through center of ball
Placement over power (especially younger groups)

benefits.immediate: Shooting technique from various angles, repetition builds confidence
benefits.longTerm: Composure in front of goal, ability to finish different types of chances

name: Quick Passing Squares
setup: 10x10m square. Player at each corner. 1-2 balls.
instructions: Pass and follow ball to next corner
Receive across body, pass with other foot
Progress: 2 balls going simultaneously, add 1-2 touch limit
Competition: which square completes most passes in 60 seconds
coachingPoints: Pass to feet with correct weight
Receive on back foot
Head up before passing (see next player)

benefits.immediate: Passing accuracy, receiving technique, movement off ball
benefits.longTerm: Quick combination play, awareness of teammates' positions

name: Creating Space (Movement Off Ball)
setup: 25x20m grid. 6v4 possession game (attackers keep ball from defenders).
instructions: Attackers must make 8 consecutive passes to score point
Encourage movement into space (check away then back)
Defenders press but don't tackle initially (passive) — then increase pressure
coachingPoints: Move before receiving (don't stand still)
Create passing angles (support behind and ahead of ball)
If space is tight, move away to drag defender and open gap

benefits.immediate: Understanding how movement creates space for teammates
benefits.longTerm: Intelligent off-ball running that opens up defenses

name: Counter-Attack Transitions
setup: 40x30m pitch. 6v6. Defending team starts with ball in own half.
instructions: Attackers win ball and immediately break forward
Goal: score within 10 seconds of winning possession
Defenders must recover quickly
Reset after goal or ball out
coachingPoints: First pass forward after winning ball (don't go backwards)
Sprint into space ahead of ball
Finish quickly (don't over-play)

benefits.immediate: Speed of thought and action on transition, direct attacking
benefits.longTerm: Recognizing counter-attack opportunities in matches

name: Small-Sided Game (Attacking Focus)
setup: 30x20m pitch. 5v5 or 6v6.
instructions: Normal game but award bonus point for: (1) Team goal from pass (not solo dribble), (2) Three consecutive passes before goal
Encourages combination play over individual efforts
coachingPoints: Look for pass before dribble
Support ball-carrier with runs
Celebrate teamwork goals

benefits.longTerm: Understanding team attacking patterns and when to pass vs. dribble

name: Rondo (Keep-Away)
setup: 10x10m grid. 6 players on outside vs. 2 defenders in middle.
instructions: Outside players keep ball with passes
Defenders try to intercept or touch ball
If defender succeeds, player who made mistake goes in middle
Progress: 2-touch limit, then 1-touch
coachingPoints: Look before receiving (know where pressure is)
Pass into space (lead teammate away from defender)
Body shape open to see whole grid

benefits.longTerm: Composure in tight spaces, ability to play out from back

name: Pass and Move Grids
setup: 20x20m grid divided into 4 squares. Players spread across grid.
instructions: Pass to teammate then move to different square
Receiver must control and pass to someone in another square
Rule: can't stay in same square after passing
Progress: 2-touch, then 1-touch
coachingPoints: Pass and move immediately (don't watch pass)
Move to space in different square
Communicate (call for ball)

benefits.longTerm: Understanding 'pass and move' principle fundamental to possession football

name: Triangle Passing
setup: Groups of 3 in triangle shape (5m apart). Multiple groups in grid.
instructions: Pass around triangle: A→B, B→C, C→A
After passing, follow ball to that position (rotate)
Progress: add defender in middle, increase speed, 1-touch passing
coachingPoints: Firm passes to feet
Receive across body (open hips)
Constant movement (don't stand still after passing)

benefits.longTerm: Triangular passing patterns used in build-up play

name: Switch Play Practice
setup: 30x20m grid. 6v6 possession. Mark 'wings' on each side (5m wide zones).
instructions: Team scores point for completing pass to teammate in opposite wing zone
Encourages switching play from congested side to open side
Defenders try to prevent switches
coachingPoints: Recognize when to switch (if one side crowded)
Quality of long pass (weight and accuracy)
Receiver in wing: check shoulder before ball arrives

benefits.immediate: Switching point of attack, long passing
benefits.longTerm: Tactical awareness of using width to stretch defenses

name: Possession Game (3 Teams Rotating)
setup: 25x25m grid. 3 teams of 4-6 players.
instructions: Two teams combine to keep ball from third team
Defending team tries to win ball or force mistake
If defenders win ball OR attacking team loses possession, that team becomes defenders
Team that kept ball longest = winners
coachingPoints: Quick passing to avoid pressure
Support ball-carrier from all angles
Transition quickly when you become defenders

benefits.immediate: Possession under pressure, quick transitions, teamwork
benefits.longTerm: Game intelligence, reading when you're in/out of possession

name: Shot-Stopping Angles
setup: Mark shooting positions at different angles (straight on, left, right) 12-15m from goal.
instructions: Shooters take turns from each position
Keeper practices positioning (adjust angle for each shot)
Focus on getting body behind ball
coachingPoints: Move off line towards shooter (narrow angle)
Set position early (before shot)
Strong base (knees bent, ready to spring)

benefits.longTerm: Angle play and understanding how to make goal smaller for shooters

name: Distribution Practice
setup: Cones placed at various distances/angles from goal.
instructions: Keeper practices throwing, rolling, and kicking to targets
Overarm throw (close), javelin throw (medium), goal kick (long)
Progress: add pressure (teammate closes down quickly)
coachingPoints: Overarm: step through and follow through to target
Javelin: side-on, point with non-throwing arm
Goal kick: strike through ball, lean back slightly

benefits.longTerm: Accurate, confident distribution under pressure

name: 1v1 with Striker
setup: Striker starts 15m out with ball. Keeper in goal.
instructions: Striker tries to dribble and score
Keeper comes off line to close down and make save
Keeper: decide when to dive at feet vs. stay up and block
coachingPoints: Come out quickly and make yourself big
Stay on feet as long as possible (force striker to decide)
If diving at feet, protect yourself and smother ball

benefits.longTerm: Confidence in one-on-one situations

name: Command Your Box (Crosses/Corners)
setup: Servers on wings. 3v3 in box. Keeper in goal.
instructions: Server crosses or takes corner
Keeper decides: come for ball (call 'KEEPER') or stay on line and organize defenders
Attackers try to score, defenders clear
coachingPoints: Call early and loud if coming for ball
Attack ball at highest point (jump across path)
If staying, position for shot and direct defenders ('push up', 'mark tight')

benefits.longTerm: Command of penalty area and organizing defense

name: World Cup (Knockout Tournament)
setup: 1v1 knockout. Each player picks a country. Winner stays on, loser out.
instructions: 1v1 in 10x10m grid with small goal
First to score wins and faces next player
Loser goes to back of queue
Last player standing = World Cup winner
coachingPoints: Encourage creativity and flair
Celebrate good play from both sides
Keep games quick (30 seconds max per round)

benefits.longTerm: Confidence in 1v1 situations, handling pressure

name: Sharks and Minnows
setup: 20x20m grid. All players with ball (minnows) except 2 sharks (no ball).
instructions: Minnows dribble across grid without losing ball
Sharks try to kick balls out of area
If ball kicked out, player becomes shark
Last minnow wins
coachingPoints: Keep ball close in traffic
Head up to see sharks
Use body to shield ball

benefits.longTerm: Protecting ball in congested areas

name: King of the Ring
setup: 15x15m grid. All players with ball inside grid.
instructions: Dribble and protect your own ball while trying to kick others' balls out
If your ball leaves grid, you're out (or do 5 toe-taps and rejoin)
Last player with ball in grid = King of the Ring
coachingPoints: Keep ball close
Use body to shield
Head up (see other players)

benefits.longTerm: Ability to maintain possession under pressure from multiple directions

name: Match Day (Small-Sided Game)
setup: 30x20m pitch. 5v5 or 6v6.
instructions: Normal game with rolling subs
Let them play (minimal stoppages)
Celebrate effort, teamwork, good play from both teams
coachingPoints: Minimal coaching (let them problem-solve)
Encourage positive communication
Rotate positions so everyone tries different roles

benefits.longTerm: Love of the game, tactical awareness from playing

name: Stretch and Chat (Debrief Circle)
setup: Circle on grass. Everyone sits.
instructions: Gentle stretches (quads, hamstrings, calves)
Go around circle: each player shares one thing they did well today
Coach highlights 2-3 moments of great teamwork/effort from session
Remind them of next session date
coachingPoints: Make it positive and inclusive
Celebrate improvement, not just goals
Ask questions ('What did you enjoy today?')

benefits.longTerm: Reflective practice, self-awareness, belonging
